Product Manager · Travel-Tech, Mobility & Fintech

Girish L. Kamkar

Product Manager — 20 years across product & finance, from 0→1 marketplaces to compliance-grade billing infrastructure

I build product and finance infrastructure at the intersection of travel, mobility, and fintech — from an AI-driven, 0→1 ride-hailing marketplace to the multi-currency billing and reconciliation systems that keep it audit-ready. 20 years across product and finance, starting my career as an accountant. Based in Bengaluru, open to India and remote roles.

Product Strategy & 0→1

Taking products from concept to launch — roadmapping, PRDs, and North Star metric design for new marketplaces and platforms.

Platform & Product Management

API-first platforms, SaaS and B2B products, booking and fulfilment engines, multi-sided marketplaces, real-time allocation systems, SLA optimisation.

GDS, OTA & NDC Integrations

Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport, and NDC content; OTA channels including Expedia, Booking.com, Ctrip, and MakeMyTrip.

Payments & Financial Systems

Payment gateway integration (Cybersource), multi-currency processing, virtual card settlement, and BSP reconciliation.

Finance & Accounts

Multi-currency accounting, inter-company billing & transfer pricing, GST/GDPR/PCI DSS compliance, AP/AR.

ERP & Financial Tools

Hands-on experience with Tally, SAP, Microsoft Dynamics, and Zoho Books across finance and reconciliation workflows.

Reconciliation & Matching Engines

Multi-provider matching logic, exception handling, aging & exposure tracking, P&L ownership, IFRS.

Marketplace, Supply & CRM Ops

Multi-sided supply networks across transport and airlines; B2B/B2C CRM; ancillary revenue merchandising.

AI, Automation & Reporting Tools

LLM-driven data extraction (ChatGPT, Gemini), conversational chatbots, n8n orchestration; SQL basics, Tableau, Power BI, MS Office (Excel, PowerPoint).

Stakeholder Management

Cross-functional leadership across engineering, finance, and vendor partners in fast-moving environments.

Projects

Selected Work
ELF / 01

Ride requests arrive via Zoho Mail and supplier web portals in inconsistent, often unstructured formats. Rather than brittle rules-based parsing, ChatGPT and Gemini serve as the extraction layer — reading free text, tables, and attachments to pull structured fields (pickup, drop-off, time, passenger, vehicle type), resolving ambiguity like relative dates and airport codes, and cross-validating outputs for accuracy.

80% AI-automated ride creation
ELF / 02

A monitoring layer flags confirmed bookings that haven't been converted into system rides, triggering automatic alerts to ops before pickups are missed.

ELF / 03

Handles routine customer and driver queries — status, ETA, modifications — conversationally, reducing live-agent load.

ELF / 04

Ride confirmations, driver details, and live trip updates delivered via WhatsApp for higher visibility than email — critical for time-sensitive travel communication.

ELF / 05

Groups overlapping ride legs or passengers — for example, same-flight arrivals — into coordinated trips, improving vehicle utilization and cost efficiency.

ELF / 06

Standardized, precise pickup/drop-off points at high-complexity venues like airports and stations, eliminating driver-passenger confusion.

ELF / 07

Rules-based automatic matching of confirmed rides to the best available driver or supplier, based on proximity, vehicle type, cost, and priority.

Sub-2s ride assignment
ELF / 08

On-demand hailing orchestrated as a visual n8n workflow: trigger → partner API call (Uber, Bolt, Lyft, Cabify, Free Now, Blacklane) → consolidates fare quotes and selects the lowest → booking → status and WhatsApp notification. Workflow-based orchestration keeps iteration on business rules fast with low engineering overhead.

ELF / 09

A single unified API supporting every product type — one-way and round-trip transfers, at-disposal hourly service, multi-day chauffeur bookings, rail ticketing, and real-time hailing — so partners integrate once and access the full product suite.

86% pre-book fulfilment 95% less manual dispatch 90% partner adoption
MYS / 01

A billing engine generating invoices in multiple currencies for B2B customers across different countries, with two compliance layers built on top.

  • GST: correct CGST/SGST/IGST tax calculation for interstate vs. intrastate transactions, HSN/SAC codes, GSTIN validation, and e-invoicing format support.
  • GDPR: consent tracking and secure handling of customer data across EU and other jurisdictions.
Multi-currency invoicing GST compliant GDPR compliant
MYS / 02

Instant invoice generation on transaction completion rather than batch processing, for both domestic and international customers. Credit notes handle cancellations and price adjustments, with jurisdiction-specific formats and tax treatment.

Real-time generation Domestic + international
MYS / 03

Validated billing data flows automatically into the core finance/ERP system — pre-transmission checks for amount matching, tax code accuracy, and duplicate detection protect books integrity and eliminate manual re-entry.

ERP data sync Pre-transmission validation
MYS / 04

Cybersource-powered payment processing with automated fraud rule sets — velocity checks, geolocation mismatch, anomaly detection — to block suspicious transactions in real time. PCI DSS compliance ensures card data is tokenized and never directly stored.

Cybersource integration PCI DSS compliant Automated fraud rules
MYS / 05

Flexible commercial terms supported via distinct ledger types, each with its own balance tracking, limit checks, aging, and settlement logic.

  • Credit: buy now, pay later, on agreed terms.
  • Deposit: pre-funded balance, drawn down per transaction.
  • Payment on the go: real-time settlement.
  • Temporary credit: short-term limit extension beyond standard credit.
4 ledger types Flexible B2B terms
MYS / 06

Matching engine pairing billed amounts against payments received (customer side) and supplier invoices against amounts owed (supplier side) — auto-closing matched transactions and surfacing only exceptions for manual review.

Customer + supplier matching Exception-based review
MYS / 07

Automated billing between related legal entities across jurisdictions — for example, India ↔ UAE — priced at arm's length to satisfy transfer pricing regulations in each market, unified under a single platform.

Multi-entity billing Transfer pricing compliant
ATL / 01

Built the reconciliation infrastructure for payments to 180+ Low-Cost Carrier (LCC) airlines, processing nearly 10,000 transactions per day. LCCs largely fall outside standard BSP participation, so payments were made via virtual cards issued through card service providers (Enett, WEX, NIUM, ConnexPay, Pax2Pay). The matching engine paired virtual card settlement data against internal booking/order records using multi-field logic — amount, reference number, date, supplier identifier — rather than single-field lookups, essential at this transaction volume.

180+ LCC airlines ~10,000 transactions/day
ATL / 02

Standardized settlement file formats and reporting cadences across multiple virtual card providers into a single reconciliation pipeline, since each provider reports differently.

ATL / 03

Auto-closed clean matches and flagged mismatches — over/under-charges, duplicate charges, unmatched or declined transactions, currency conversion differences, and timing gaps between booking and airline processing — for finance team review.

ATL / 04

Monitored unmatched or disputed transactions over time to control financial exposure across the full 180+ supplier relationship base.

180+ airline reconciliation automation
CAL / 01

A dual-facing B2B and B2C reservation platform, integrated with all three major GDS platforms — Amadeus, Sabre, and Travelport — for live airline fare/seat inventory and hotel availability/rates. Multi-GDS coverage broadens supplier reach and content depth, since GDS strength varies by region and airline/hotel relationships.

B2B + B2C 3 GDS platforms
CAL / 02

Integrated NDC (New Distribution Capability) — the airline industry's XML-based standard enabling richer, more personalized content distribution such as branded fares, seat maps, and bundled ancillaries beyond traditional GDS/EDIFACT messaging. NDC sits alongside GDS, giving access to enhanced airline content while GDS remains the broader distribution backbone.

CAL / 03

Manages B2B agent/corporate accounts and B2C customer profiles — booking history, preferences, communication logs — supporting sales and support workflows beyond the individual transaction.

CAL / 04

Travel-specific finance layer covering BSP reconciliation for airline ticket sales, commission tracking per booking, supplier payables, agent/customer receivables, and multi-currency handling tied to fare rules — turning bookings into revenue recognition and settlement.

CAL / 05

Sale of add-on products beyond the base booking — seat selection, baggage, meals, travel insurance, hotel upgrades — presented and priced at the right point in the booking flow across both B2B and B2C channels, a key margin driver in travel commerce.

Work Experience

Career Timeline

Led the 0→1 build of a global ride-hailing and ground transportation marketplace, including the Multi-Model API for payments, and owned strategy, distribution, and supply integrations end to end.

  • Managed strategic API integrations with Tier 1 global OTAs — Expedia, Booking.com, Ctrip, MakeMyTrip, Klook, ETG, TripAdvisor, and Amadeus — enabling seamless multi-channel distribution.
  • Led supply-side API integrations with 9 global service providers, including Bolt, Uber, Cabify, and Lyft, enabling cross-geography fulfilment and expanding platform supply coverage.

Owned B2B payments and reconciliation workflows for a global travel payments network, working across multi-currency settlement and supplier billing. Delivered 180+ airline reconciliation system automation across the global supply network.

Co-founded Fly Meridians, driving the business to 5× revenue growth.

Held combined product and finance leadership responsibilities at an early-stage company.

Worked across GDS integrations and BSP reconciliation, spanning both product and finance functions over multiple tenures.

Started early career as an Accountant, learning accounting from the ground up, and grew into the role of Branch Accountant before exiting — alongside broader exposure to reservations, ground operations, and DMC/supplier coordination.
